mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors price in future growth.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong consumer spending supports economic growth
failure_condition: If RSAFS shows consistent growth but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are predominantly negative, or the Rank IC for RSAFS falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic contraction, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising federal funds rates and declining housing starts indicates a tightening monetary policy impacting interest-sensitive sectors, which dampens economic activity and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.1% and HOUST falls > 5% MoM
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is sensitive to interest rates
failure_condition: If the specified trigger condition is met, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of occurrences, or the combined signal shows no predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in industrial production indicates a robust manufacturing sector and increasing productive capacity, signaling economic expansion that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5% MoM, but S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating year-over-year GDP growth signifies a strong and expanding economy, which typically leads to higher corporate earnings and investor confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP is a primary measure of economic growth
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth consistently exceeds 2% YoY, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.1) during these periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent increases in nonfarm payrolls reflect a healthy and expanding labor market, indicating strong employment and income growth, which underpins consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Job growth is a key economic indicator
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows sustained positive growth, but the S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are consistently negative, or the Rank IC for PAYEMS falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent and accelerating month-over-month increases in the Consumer Price Index signal rising inflationary pressures, which often prompts central bank tightening and erodes real corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.4% MoM for 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Inflation can lead to higher interest rates
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL meets the trigger condition, but S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong retail sales and low unemployment indicates a robust consumer-driven economy with ample disposable income, fostering an environment of strong corporate earnings growth and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If both RSAFS growth is positive and UNRATE is below 5%, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 35% of such periods, or the interaction term lacks statistical significance.
